title: ficus nemoralis var. fieldingii
intro: Ficus nemoralis var. fieldingii, also known as the Fielding's banyan, is a variety of fig tree native to India and Southeast Asia. It has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to the presence of compounds such as gallic acid and tannins. The plant has been reported to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating digestive issues, and aiding in the treatment of respiratory problems. Ficus nemoralis var. fieldingii is often used in herbal preparations, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, where it is combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
